notes: Godard cited Rashomon's unreliable narration as liberating for his own storytelling.
Coppola acknowledged Seven Samurai as a template for group loyalty and betrayal.
Gordon Willis and Coppola drew on Citizen Kane's low-angle lighting for The Godfather.
Kubrick studied Gregg Toland's deep focus technique extensively before 2001.
Lynch acknowledged Vertigo's psychological doubling as a key reference for Mulholland Drive.
Wong Kar-wai's fragmented editing in Chungking Express draws on Godard's jump cuts.
Tarkovsky described The Seventh Seal as confirming cinema's capacity for philosophy.
Malick's fragmentary childhood memories in Tree of Life echo Tarkovsky's Mirror.
Kubrick's Full Metal Jacket uses Seven Samurai's group dynamics in a war context.
Days of Heaven's visual poetry is a direct development of Badlands' approach.
Varda acknowledged The 400 Blows' influence on her portrayal of marginal youth.
Wong Kar-wai has cited Jeanne Dielman's temporal realism as an influence.
Kiarostami's minimalist rural films owe a clear debt to Satyajit Ray's Apu Trilogy.
Orson Welles' own Touch of Evil continues his exploration of deep-focus grammar.
Scorsese has repeatedly cited The Godfather as the model for Goodfellas.
Godard's Vivre Sa Vie continues Breathless' experiment in location handheld shooting.
Akerman cited Persona as foundational for her exploration of women's inner lives.
Amarcord revisits La Dolce Vita's episodic portrait of Italian social life.
Lynch's Mulholland Drive shares 8½'s structure of a protagonist confusing fantasy and reality.
Blue Velvet develops the surrealist horror Eraserhead established.
Sembène's Xala extends the political critique he launched with Black Girl.
Close-Up's play with reality and fiction owes much to Rashomon's shifting perspectives.
Taste of Cherry's contemplative structure follows Stalker's slow existential road movie.
The Tree of Life's battlefield imagery consciously echoes Apocalypse Now.
Hitchcock deepened Rear Window's voyeurism theme in Vertigo.
The Godfather's code of silence mirrors the samurai honour code Yojimbo examines.
Mirror's disorienting time structure anticipates the fragmented consciousness of Eyes Wide Shut.
Varda's own One Sings builds on the feminist wandering established in Cléo from 5 to 7.
Aparajito is a direct sequel continuing Pather Panchali's portrait of Apu's growth.
Taxi Driver's visual debt to Citizen Kane appears in its nocturnal low-angle city shots.
